headline_verdict: Coherent architecture, but file-count justification requires boundary review
primary_review_question: Do we need 326 files to pull off this taxonomy scanning and swarming solution?

file_economy.score_meaning: Mostly justified; review zero-method and one-method boundaries before scaling.

category_rows.notes: Justified when API, browser, route, and integration boundaries stay navigable.
Justified when command behavior and operator entry points stay isolated.
Justified when each module owns one runtime responsibility.
Justified when report rendering stays isolated from scoring and healing.
Review for helper fragmentation and repeated one-method modules.
Justified when evaluator pieces remain independently testable.
Justified when scanning, extraction, evidence, and healing stay separable.
Justified when tests protect coherence governance and report contracts.
Justified when decomposition keeps agent work packets small and governable.
Justified only for wrappers, config, registry, boundary, or executable surfaces.
Review whether the single method has durable semantic weight.

canonical_surface_map.legacy_or_alternate_surfaces: bin/taxonomy-report.js, bin/taxonomy-scan.js, bin/verify-scan-fixture.js, bin/verify-scan-validator.js, bin/verify-scan.js, bin/verify-scan.test.js
canonical_surface_map.relationship: canonical renderer with CLI and verification surfaces
partial overlap with run progress and summary reporting
legacy command redirected to canonical report
canonical_surface_map.decision: keep redirect only if needed; retire unused alternate surfaces

canonical_surface_map.relationship: operational overlap between expected taxonomy healing and direct anchor mutation
canonical_surface_map.decision: choose mutation path per governance policy
classify as canonical worker-local or retire

residue_queue.reason: Compatibility shell remains after canonical report surface was introduced.
residue_queue.decision: Keep only while documented, otherwise retire.

residue_queue.reason: Story-report naming overlaps with canonical Codebase Story Review narrative.
residue_queue.decision: Retire, redirect, or justify as a distinct package artifact.

final_verdict:
The studio codebase now tells a coherent taxonomy story. The scan reviewed 326 files, found file anchors on all 326, found all 643 expected method anchors, and reported 100/100 coherence.

That means the codebase is semantically trustworthy at the taxonomy level. However, the file count should not be treated as automatically justified simply because coherence is 100/100.

The next review layer must also detect legacy idea residue: older scripts, reports, commands, wrappers, and documents that may remain after newer canonical solutions replaced their purpose. A file can be coherent and still be obsolete.

The recommendation is to accept the taxonomy coherence result, preserve the responsibility-first architecture, run a file-economy review pass, and keep the canonical-surface/residue review active before scaling the pattern to larger Python or LLC codebases.
